nodes.name: Lesser roadrunner
nodes.summary: Lesser roadrunner（Lesser roadrunner，学名 Geococcyx velox），分类上属于鹃形目，并归入杜鹃科，在图谱中标记为无危（LC）物种，已关联13个分布地点节点。
locations: 約翰·安德烈亞斯·瓦格納

links.target: loc-約翰-安德烈亞斯-瓦格納-0dddcb93
links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: The lesser roadrunner (Geococcyx velox) is a large, long-legged bird that is a member of the cuckoo family, Cuculidae

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: It inhabits open ground areas, with scrub and thorny bushes. They can be found in higher elevations of stratovolcanoes such as Conchagua, San Miguel, Santa Ana and San Salvador, in semi-open areas above the treeline.{{cite book|last1=Dickey|first1=D

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: It inhabits open ground areas, with scrub and thorny bushes. They can be found in higher elevations of stratovolcanoes such as Conchagua, San Miguel, Santa Ana and San Salvador, in semi-open areas above the treeline.{{cite book|last1=Dickey|first1=D

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: J.|title=The birds of El Salvador|year=1938|volume=23|publisher=Field Museum of Natural History|url=https://www.biodiversitylibrary.org/page/2779758#page/234/mode/1up}} They also adapt to cultivated land such as henequen and corn fields. Its breeding range is in southwestern Mexico, north into the western side of the Sierra Madre Occidental range, along with northern Central America and a disjunct range in the northern Yucatán Peninsula. It overlaps with the range of the greater roadrunner in a small area in the states of Sonora, Sinaloa, Jalisco, and Michoacán.{{cite book|last1=Howell|first1=Steve N
